title: Methods should avoid containing selector parameters
description: Methods with boolean selector parameters that determine execution paths reduce code clarity and maintainability. Instead of using boolean flags to control method behavior, consider creating separate methods or using polymorphism to make the intent more explicit.
recommendation: Replace boolean selector parameters with separate methods or use object parameters to make method intent clearer.

noncompliant: function process(data, isAdvanced) { if (isAdvanced) { /* complex */ } else { /* simple */ } }
function render(content, useCache) { return useCache ? getCached() : generate(); }
function send(message, urgent) { urgent ? sendImmediately() : queue(); }
compliant: function send(message, options = {}) { options.urgent ? sendImmediately() : queue(); }

checks.message: Method contains boolean selector parameter - consider splitting into separate methods
Arrow function uses boolean selector parameter - consider refactoring for clarity
Method behavior controlled by boolean parameter - consider separate methods for each path
